title: Arrays & Strings
tips: Master the pattern, not the problem. Every array problem is either two pointers, sliding window, or hash map.

title: Hash Maps & Stacks
tips: Hash maps turn O(n²) into O(n). Stack is the answer whenever you need 'last seen' context.

title: Binary Search & Sorting
tips: Binary search works on any monotonic condition. The answer space is often more powerful than array search.

title: Linked Lists
tips: Always use a dummy node to handle edge cases. Fast-slow pointers detect cycles and find midpoints.

title: Trees - DFS
tips: Every tree problem is either DFS (recursion) or BFS (queue). DFS is more common in interviews.

title: Trees - BFS & Tries
tips: BFS for level-by-level. Trie for prefix matching and autocomplete.

title: Graphs - DFS & BFS
tips: DFS explores deep, BFS explores wide. Always track visited nodes. Union-Find for dynamic connectivity.

title: Heaps & Intervals
tips: Min-heap for K largest. Max-heap for K smallest. Two heaps for median.

title: Backtracking
tips: Template: for each choice -> make choice -> recurse -> undo. Prune impossible branches.

title: Dynamic Programming - 1D
tips: dp[i] = answer for first i elements. Find transition from dp[i-1], dp[i-2]. Start with recursion, convert to tabulation.

title: Dynamic Programming - 2D
tips: dp[i][j] = answer for first i of A and first j of B. Fill row by row, use previous row for space optimization.

title: Review & Mock Interviews
tips: Do 5+ mock interviews. Practice explaining code out loud. Focus on your weakest patterns.
